findings.evidence: New lemma at `DiagonalizableGroupFunctoriality.lean:69`; Mathlib defines `MonoidAlgebra.mapDomainBialgHom (f : M →* N)` at `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/Bialgebra/MonoidAlgebra.lean:68` and `MonoidAlgebra.mapDomain_single` for arbitrary `r` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/MonoidAlgebra/MapDomain.lean:57`.
findings.fix: Generalize the public lemma to monoids, preferably with arbitrary coefficient `r`, e.g. for `[Monoid M] [Monoid N] (φ : M →* N) (m : M) (r : R)` prove `MonoidAlgebra.mapDomainBialgHom R φ (MonoidAlgebra.single m r) = MonoidAlgebra.single (φ m) r`; or make this helper private if only the `single _ 1` case is needed internally.
findings.issue: `mapDomainBialgHom_single_one` assumes `[CommGroup G] [CommGroup G']`, but the statement is just the action of `MonoidAlgebra.mapDomainBialgHom` on a basis element and does not use commutativity or inverses.

summary: One new public helper is exported at the diagonalizable-group/commutative-group level even though the underlying Mathlib API is monoid-level; either generalize it or keep it private.
